meta.dc:title: Public Law 119–65: To amend the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act to improve the administration of the Marine Debris Foundation, to amend the Marine Debris Act to improve the administration of the Marine Debris Program of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, and for other purposes.
meta.dc:creator: National Archives and Records Administration
Office of the Federal Register
meta.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

longTitle.officialTitle:
To amend the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act to improve the administration of the Marine Debris Foundation, to amend the Marine Debris Act to improve the administration of the Marine Debris Program of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, and for other purposes.
Dec. 26, 2025
[S. 216]

enactingFormula.i: Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representa­tives of the United States of America in Congress assembled,

section.heading: SHORT TITLE.
section.content: This Act may be cited as the “Save Our Seas 2.0 Amendments Act”.

section.heading: MODIFICATIONS TO THE MARINE DEBRIS PROGRAM OF THE NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION.

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: The Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449) is amended—

paragraph.content: by inserting before section 3 the following:“Subtitle A—NOAA And Coast Guard Programs” ; and

by redesignating sections 3 through 6
33 USC 1952–1955.as sections 101 through 104, respectively.

subsection.heading: Grants, Cooperative Agreements, Contracts, and Other Agreements.—
subsection.chapeau: Section 101(d) of the Marine Debris Act (33 U.S.C. 1952(d)), as redesignated by this Act, is amended—

paragraph.content: in the subsection heading by striking “AND CONTRACTS” and inserting “CONTRACTS, AND OTHER AGREEMENTS”;
in paragraph (1) by striking “and contracts” and inserting “, contracts, and other agreements”;

clause.content: by striking “part of the” and inserting “part of a”; and

subparagraph.content: in subparagraph (C) in the matter preceding clause (i) by inserting “and except as provided in subparagraph (B)” after “subparagraph (A)”; and

paragraph.content: by adding at the end the following:“(7) In-kind contributions.—With respect to any project carried out pursuant to a contract or other agreement entered into under paragraph (1) that is not a cooperative agreement 139 STAT. 1987 or an agreement to provide financial assistance in the form of a grant, the Under Secretary may contribute on an in-kind basis the portion of the costs of the project that the Under Secretary determines represents the amount of benefit the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration derives from the project.” .

section.heading: MODIFICATIONS TO THE MARINE DEBRIS FOUNDATION.

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content:
Subtitle B of title I of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224) is transferred to appear after section 104 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449),
33 USC 1961–1969. as redesignated by this Act.

subsection.heading: Status of Foundation.—
subsection.content:
Section 111(a) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred
33 USC 1961.by this Act, is amended, in the second sentence, by striking “organization” and inserting “corporation”.

subsection.heading: Purposes.—
subsection.chapeau: Section 111(b) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred and redesignated by this Act, is amended—

paragraph.content: in paragraph (3) by inserting “Indian Tribes,” after “Tribal governments,”; and
in paragraph (4) by striking “title II” and inserting “subtitle C”.

paragraph.heading: Appointment, vacancies, and removal.—
paragraph.chapeau:
Section 112(b) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this
33 USC 1962.Act, is amended—

subparagraph.content: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (5) as paragraphs (2) through (6) respectively;

by inserting before paragraph (2), as redesignated, the following:“(1) Recommendations of board regarding appointments.—For appointments made under paragraph (2), the Board shall submit to the Under Secretary recommendations on candidates for appointment.” ;

subparagraph.chapeau: in paragraph (2), as redesignated, in the matter preceding subparagraph (A)—

clause.content: by striking “and considering” and inserting “considering”; and
by inserting “and with the approval of the Secretary of Commerce,” after “by the Board,”;

subparagraph.content: by amending paragraph (3), as redesignated, to read as follows:“(3) Terms.—Any Director appointed under paragraph (2) shall be appointed for a term of 6 years.” ;
in paragraph (4)(A), as redesignated, by inserting “with the approval of the Secretary of Commerce” after “the Board”; and

clause.content: by inserting “the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development,” after “Service,”; and
by inserting “and with the approval of the Secretary of Commerce” after “EPA Administrator”.

paragraph.heading: General powers.—
paragraph.chapeau: Section 112(g) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this Act, is amended—139 STAT. 1988

subparagraph.content: in paragraph (1)(A) by striking “officers and employees” and inserting “the initial officers and employees”; and
in paragraph (2)(B)(i) by striking “its chief operating officer” and inserting “the chief executive officer of the Foundation”.

paragraph.heading: Chief executive officer.—
paragraph.content:
Section 112 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this Act, is amended by adding at the end the following:“(h) Chief Executive Officer.—“(1) Appointment; removal; review.—The Board shall appoint and review the performance of, and may remove, the chief executive officer of the Foundation. “(2) Powers.—The chief executive officer of the Foundation may appoint, remove, and review the performance of any officer or employee of the Foundation.”
.

subsection.heading: Powers of Foundation.—
subsection.chapeau:
Section 113(c)(1) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this
33 USC 1963. Act, is amended in the matter preceding subparagraph (A)—

paragraph.content: by inserting “nonprofit” before “corporation”; and
by striking “acting as a trustee” and inserting “formed”.

subsection.heading: Principal Office.—
subsection.content: Section 113 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this Act, is amended by adding at the end the following:“(g) Principal Office.—The Board shall locate the principal office of the Foundation in the National Capital Region, as such term is defined in section 2674(f)(2) of title 10, United States Code, or a coastal shoreline community.” .

subsection.heading: Best Practices; Rule of Construction.—
subsection.content:
Section 113 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this Act and amended by subsection (e), is further amended by adding at the end the following:“(h) Best Practices.—“(1) In general.—The Foundation shall develop and implement best practices for conducting outreach to Indian Tribes and Tribal Governments. “(2) Requirements.—The best practices developed under paragraph (1) shall—“(A)
Process.include a process to support technical assistance and capacity building to improve outcomes; and “(B) promote an awareness of programs and grants available under this Act.
“(i) Rule of Construction.—Nothing in this Act may be construed—“(1) to satisfy any requirement for government-to-government consultation with Tribal Governments; or “(2) to affect or modify any treaty or other right of any Tribal Government.”
.

subsection.heading: Authorization of Appropriations.—
subsection.chapeau:
Section 118(a) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred by this Act,
33 USC 1968.is amended—

paragraph.content: in paragraph (1), by inserting “and $2,000,000 for fiscal year 2025” after “through 2024”; and

in paragraph (2), by striking “and State and local government agencies” and inserting “, State and local government agencies, regional organizations, Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, and foreign governments”.139 STAT. 1989

subsection.heading: Reauthorization.—
subsection.content:
Section 9(a) of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449)
33 USC 1958.is amended by striking “for” the first place it appears and all that follows through “carrying out” and inserting “for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2029 for carrying out”.

subsection.heading: Save Our Seas 2.0 Act.—
subsection.content:
Subtitle C of title I of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224)
33 USC 1971–1977.is transferred to appear after section 119 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449) as transferred and redesignated by this Act.

subsection.heading: Marine Debris Act.—
subsection.chapeau: The Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449) is amended—

paragraph.content:
by transferring sections 7, 8, 9 (as amended),
33 USC 1981–1984.and 10 to appear after section 127, as transferred by this Act, and redesignated as sections 131, 132, 133, and 134, respectively; and
by inserting before section 131, as so transferred and redesignated, the following:“Subtitle D—Administration” .

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau:
Section 131 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred and redesignated by
33 USC 1981.this Act, is amended—

paragraph.content: by striking paragraph (1);
by redesignating paragraphs (2), (3), (4), (5), (6), and (7) as paragraphs (5), (6), (7), (11), (12), and (13), respectively;

by inserting before paragraph (5), as so redesignated, the following:“(1) Circular economy.—The term ‘circular economy’ has the meaning given such term in section 2 of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224). “(2) Coastal shoreline community.—The term ‘coastal shoreline community’ means a city or county directly adjacent to the open ocean, major estuaries, or the Great Lakes. “(3) EPA administrator.—The term ‘EPA Administrator’ has the meaning given such term in section 2 of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224). “(4) Indian tribe.—The term ‘Indian Tribe’ has the meaning given that term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304).” ;

by inserting after paragraph (13), as so redesignated, the following:“(14) Tribal government.—The term ‘Tribal Government’ means the recognized governing body of any Indian or Alaska 139 STAT. 1990 Native Tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, individually identified (including parenthetically) in the list published most recently as of the date of the enactment of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Amendments Act pursuant to section 104 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 (25 U.S.C. 5131). “(15) Tribal organization.—The term ‘Tribal organization’ has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304). “(16) Under secretary.—The term ‘Under Secretary’ has the meaning given such term in section 2 of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224).” ; and

subparagraph.content: by redesignating subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) as subparagraphs (C), (D), and (E); and
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following:“(B) Indian Tribe;” .

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content:
Section 2(7) of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224)
33 USC 1981.is transferred to section 131 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), inserted after paragraph (7) (as redesignated), and redesignated as paragraph (8).

paragraph.heading: Redesignation.—
paragraph.content:
Section 2 of the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act (Public Law 116–224)
33 USC 4201.is amended by redesignating paragraphs (8) through (11) as paragraphs (7) through (10), respectively.

subsection.heading: Non-Federal Funds.—
subsection.content:
Paragraph (8)(D) of section 131 of the Marine Debris Act (Public Law 109–449), as transferred and redesignated by this Act,
33 USC 1981.is amended by striking “(as defined in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304))”.

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content:
Sections 1 and 2 of the Marine Debris Act, sections 101, 102, and 104 of the Marine Debris Act, as redesignated by this Act, and section 133 of the Marine Debris Act, as transferred and so redesignated by this Act,
33 USC 1951 and note, 1952, 1953, 1955, 1983.are amended by striking “Administrator” and inserting “Under Secretary”.

subsection.heading: Section 103.—
subsection.chapeau:
Section 103 of the Marine
33 USC 1954.Debris Act is amended by—

paragraph.content: striking “Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration” and inserting “Under Secretary”;
striking “Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency” and inserting “EPA Administrator”; and
in subsection (e)(3) by striking “section 3” and inserting “section 101”.

subsection.heading: Section 123.—
subsection.content:
Section 123 of the Marine Debris Act, as transferred and so redesignated by this Act,
33 USC 1973.is amended by striking “title I” and inserting “subtitle B”.

subsection.heading: Section 133.—
subsection.content:
Section 133 of the Marine Debris Act, as transferred and so redesignated by this Act,
33 USC 1983.is amended by striking “sections 3, 5, and 6” and inserting “sections 101, 103, and 104”.

subsection.heading: Section 134.—
subsection.content:
Section 134 of the Marine Debris Act, as transferred and so redesignated by this Act,
33 USC 1984.is amended by striking 139 STAT. 1991 “Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency” and inserting “EPA Administrator”.

subsection.heading: Tribal Government.—
subsection.content:
Subtitle A of the Marine Debris Act, as designated in this Act,
33 USC 1952.is amended by striking “tribal government” and inserting “Tribal Government”.

legislativeHistory.heading: LEGISLATIVE HISTORY—S. 216:
legislativeHistory.note: SENATE REPORTS: ┐No. 119–12 (Comm. on Commerce, Science, and Transportation).
